agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive 2025 Homeless Count report for Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a report and presentation on the 2025 Homeless Count for Vancouver. This is an information item only — no bylaw or regulation is being proposed. The report provides data on homelessness in the region, which may inform future policy decisions that could affect businesses, particularly those in the Hotel & Accommodation and Retail sectors. No speakers are allowed during this agenda item per the Procedure Bylaw.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	2025 Homeless Count – Vancouver
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
Presentation
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 Cultural Grants for Operating & Spaces
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed 2026 Cultural Grants that would provide funding for arts and cultural organizations in Burnaby. The grants cover two areas: Operating support (for day-to-day costs) and Cultural Spaces (for facilities and venues). Some parts of this proposal need at least two-thirds of all council members to vote 'yes' to pass, as required by Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter — a rule that demands a supermajority for certain funding decisions. This is not yet approved; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on this proposal — if approved, it could affect eligibility or funding for your cultural organization.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	2026 Cultural Grants (Balance of Operating, Cultural Spaces)
Recommendations A to C, E and G require at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants for Spring 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to award grants to community sport events happening in Vancouver in Spring 2026. This decision requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote 'yes' — a higher bar than a simple majority. If approved, the grants would help local organizers cover costs for events that bring people together and promote physical activity. The exact grant amounts and which events would receive funding are not detailed in this agenda item.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed — watch for the council vote to see if your event or organization could apply for future grants.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants – Spring 2026 Intake
Recommendation A requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby property tax distribution for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on how to divide the total property tax levy among different classes of properties (like homes, businesses, and industry) for the 2026 tax year. This decision will determine how much of the overall tax burden falls on commercial and residential properties. A proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for each class. The outcome directly affects how much property tax your business will pay next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will set your 2026 property tax rate.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	2026 Property Taxation: Distribution of Property Tax Levy
4a. Budget Resolution
4b. Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 property tax exemptions for non-profits
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a report that recommends granting property tax exemptions for 2025 to certain non-profit organizations in Burnaby. A proposed bylaw would allow eligible charities, churches, and community groups to pay less or no property tax. This is not yet approved — council will debate and decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now. Watch for the council decision — if approved, it will affect which non-profits get tax relief.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.	2025 Property Tax Exemptions
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Coastal Flood Management Policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Coastal Flood Management Policy. This policy would set new rules for building and development in areas of Burnaby that are at risk of flooding from the ocean. If approved, property owners in these flood-risk zones may face new requirements for building elevations, flood-proofing, and insurance. The goal is to protect people and property from rising sea levels and storm surges. The report is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting, but no decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and public hearing dates — this policy could affect your property if you own or develop land near the coast.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Insurance & Finance
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.	Coastal Flood Management Policy
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a recommendation (Recommendation B) regarding the Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board's 2025 Annual Report. This recommendation requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote in favour, as stated in Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter. The Vancouver Charter is the city's main governing law. If passed, this would approve or adopt the report and its recommendations. The decision is not final yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.	Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board – Annual Report 2025
Recommendation B requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of theVancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on public plaza alcohol sites for 2026-2029
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to choose which public plazas in Burnaby will allow alcohol consumption from 2026 through 2029. This is part of the Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program. If approved, the program would let people drink alcohol in certain outdoor public spaces during specific hours and dates. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on the proposed sites at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near potential plaza sites should pay attention, as this could affect foot traffic, noise, and local business operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if your business is near a proposed plaza site.

key_dates.label: Burnaby public plaza alcohol program — council vote on proposed sites

agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.	Sites for 2026-2029 Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby seeks grant to speed up development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider applying for a provincial grant through the Local Government Development Approvals Program. If approved, the funding would help the city hire more staff and upgrade its systems to process building and development permits faster. This is not a final decision yet — council will vote on whether to submit the application at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for council's decision on whether to apply for the grant.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.	Funding Application to Local Government Development Approvals Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend housing rezoning conditions
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on miscellaneous amendments that would change the conditions attached to various housing rezoning approvals. These are proposed changes to the rules developers must follow when they get permission to rezone land for housing. The exact details of the amendments are not specified in the agenda item, but the decision could affect how housing projects are built or what conditions they must meet. The item is listed for discussion at an upcoming council meeting, with no public speakers allowed per the procedure bylaw.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — these amendments could change conditions on your rezoning application or future housing project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
10.	Miscellaneous Amendments Concerning Various Housing Rezoning Conditions
(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Text amendment to CD-1 (839) bylaw for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed text amendment to the Comprehensive Development (CD-1) Bylaw No. 13493 for two properties: 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue. A CD-1 bylaw is a special zoning rule that applies only to a specific site. This amendment would change the rules for what can be built or done on these properties. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend the public hearing to have your say on this proposed bylaw change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 rezoning at 4088 Granville Street, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 4088 Granville Street. A 'CD-1' (Comprehensive Development) rezoning would change the rules for what can be built on that specific property, allowing a different type of development than the current zoning permits. This is a first step — the bylaw has not been approved yet, and council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you own property nearby or have an interest in this site, consider attending or submitting comments before the vote.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 3803-3823 West 10th Ave and 2553 Highbury St
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 3803-3823 West 10th Avenue and 2553 Highbury Street. If approved, the rezoning would change the land-use rules (called a CD-1 bylaw amendment) for these sites. The proposal also includes changes to the city's main zoning bylaw and sign bylaw. This is a first reading — the decision is not final yet.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 310 East 14th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 310 East 14th Avenue. This is called a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) rezoning, which means the city would create a special set of rules just for this property, instead of using the standard zoning rules. The draft bylaw would change the official Zoning and Development Bylaw. This is a proposal only — it has not been approved yet. The public hearing is part of the council meeting agenda, but no speakers are scheduled for this item under the Procedure Bylaw.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this rezoning — it could affect nearby development or property values.

agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby amenity cost charge bylaw and density bonus updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider updates to the Amenity Cost Charge (ACC) bylaw and Density Bonus (Bill 16) rules. An ACC is a fee the city charges new development projects to help pay for community amenities like parks, community centres, and libraries. A density bonus lets developers build more units in exchange for providing affordable housing or other public benefits. The proposed changes would update how these charges and bonuses are calculated. A required public consultation on these changes is coming up. This could affect the cost and feasibility of new development projects in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public consultation dates and attend to have your say on how these charges affect your project costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
16.	2026 Financing Growth Update – Required Consultation for Amenity Cost Charge By law and Bill 16 Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning Updates
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed bylaw: Trounce Alley & Blood Alley Square maintenance costs for 2025
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners in the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square area for the 2025 maintenance costs of those public spaces. This is a 'local improvement' project, meaning the costs are shared among the properties that directly benefit from the upkeep. The bylaw would allow the city to collect these costs through property taxes. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own property in the Trounce Alley or Blood Alley Square area.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 maintenance costs for the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square Local Improvement Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to assess property costs for East Hastings parking project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge certain property owners in the East Hastings Street area for the costs of a shared parking project in 2025. The bylaw would 'assess real property' — meaning it would add a special fee or tax to specific properties to pay for the collective parking project. This is not yet approved; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending to voice your support or concerns about the proposed parking assessment.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 costs for the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a Housing Agreement for the property at 320-360 West 2nd Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in how the building will be used — for example, requiring a certain number of affordable rental units. This agreement has not been approved yet; Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 6.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amend housing agreement for Prior Street, Malkin Avenue, Station Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend an existing Housing Agreement for properties at 450-496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that sets rules for how the housing is used — often to keep it affordable or for specific types of tenants. This amendment would change the terms of that existing agreement. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 5.	A By-law to authorize the amendment of a Housing Agreement Authorized by By-law No. 14507 (450 – 496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue and 1002 Station Street)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on amending CD-1 bylaw for 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the existing Comprehensive Development (CD-1) bylaw for the properties at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue. A CD- bylaw is a special zoning rule that applies only to a specific site. This amendment could change what is allowed to be built or done on that property. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting. Councillor Montague has noted they need to review the proceedings before they are eligible to vote on this matter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own or operate a business at this location.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	A By-law to amend CD-1 (198) By-law No. 6254 (1700-1750 West 75th Avenue)
(Councillor Montague will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 1710-1730 East Pender Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 1710-1730 East Pender Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows the city to set specific rules for a particular property, rather than following the standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect property use and development potential in the area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1710-1730 East Pender Street)
(Councillor Maloney, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 6486 Chester Street to CD-1 in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 6486 Chester Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a special zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (6486 Chester Street)
(Councillors Bligh, Fry and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 1302-1318 East 12th Avenue to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1302-1318 East 12th Avenue to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. This is a first step — the bylaw has not been approved yet, and Council will debate and decide on it at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you own property nearby or have an interest in this development, attend the hearing to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1302 - 1318 East 12th Avenue)
(Councillors Klassen, Maloney, Montague, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street. A 'CD-1 Rezoning' means the land would be reclassified as a Comprehensive Development District, which allows the city to set custom rules for what can be built there. This is a first step — the proposal has not been approved yet. Council will consider a referral report that starts the public process. If you own a business or property in this area, this could affect future development or land use.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to give input on the proposed rezoning before Council makes a final decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
Referral Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 rezoning for 888 West 8th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for the property at 888 West 8th Avenue. The owner wants to change the land-use designation to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone, which is a special zoning category that allows for a mix of uses not normally permitted under standard rules. This proposal would allow a new development on the site. Council will consider the referral report at an upcoming meeting before deciding whether to move forward with public hearings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888 West 8th Avenue
Referral Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fast-track rental housing and small hotels in new mixed-use zone
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to create a new 'Mixed-Use Commercial District' that would fast-track approvals for rental housing and small hotels. If approved, the new rules would allow developers to build rental apartments and small hotels (under a certain size) more quickly, without needing a full rezoning process for each project. This is not law yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting. Business owners in the hotel and development sectors should watch for the decision, as it could change where and how quickly new rental housing and small hotels can be built in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting date and consider submitting comments or attending to voice your support or concerns about the proposed fast-track zone.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	Fast-Tracking Rental Housing and Small Hotels – New Mixed-Use Commercial District
Referral Report
